title: Host the full supported Pi RPC surface on AgentSessionRuntime
description: Replace the narrow prompt, steer, followUp, abort adapter with an in-process AgentSessionRuntime host compatible with stock Pi RPC semantics. Cover prompt and images, queue modes, state and messages, model and max thinking control, compaction and retry, session stats, commands, entries and tree cursors, naming, new, switch, fork, clone, import and export, bash where policy permits, extension UI request and response, agent_settled, entry_appended, and session replacement rebinding. Maintain typed capability discovery and conformance fixtures against the pinned Pi RPC protocol rather than importing Cacophony types.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Bound protocol event and response serialization before allocation
description: Resource-bound finding from bd-62e2c5. ConnectionWriter.send in src/server.ts calls encodeLine and Buffer.from on the complete response or SDK event before checking maxOutboundBytesPerConnection. Model message events are not journal-record bounded, and mapped Pi event payloads can contain full messages, tool results, or provider error data. Therefore the advertised outbound bound controls only queued bytes after an unbounded JSON serialization and allocation. Define per-event and per-response payload limits, use bounded serialization or deliberate truncation and typed overflow behavior, prevent non-serializable SDK payloads from disrupting session delivery, and add adversarial tests proving a large event cannot grow process memory or affect other sessions.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Audit Cacophony integration gaps for standalone Pi Daemon
description: Completed coordinated Cacophony-side consumer audit for Harry's 2026-07-14 Pi Daemon review. Current Cacophony main has standalone host config/preference, sidecar supervision, an exec wrapper, a status endpoint, and a bounded low-level UDS client, but no pico-daemon agent type or create/message/attach/health/lifecycle wiring. Findings sent to bd-62e2c5 owner include explicit atomic snapshot-plus-stream attach, durable CRUD for dormant sessions, async message acceptance and request lookup, full AgentSessionRuntime/RPC control parity, neutral whole-service bearer auth, REST and NDJSON equivalence, APC/ACP adapter work, and a neutral stock-Pi or pico-style attach client. Cacophony-only integration remains outside this repository.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-2

title: Establish a current Pi SDK acquisition and compatibility policy
description: The repository pins Pi 0.80.3 while current Pi 0.80.6 adds AgentSessionRuntime maturity, agent_settled, entry_appended, waitForIdle and max thinking needed by the target API, and the configured npm mirror does not yet expose 0.80.6. Define a reproducible supported acquisition source, upgrade without floating dependencies, add compile and behavioral compatibility probes for public SDK and Pi RPC fixtures, map new events, accept max thinking additively, and document upgrade cadence and rollback. Keep the no-child-process multiplex acceptance while enabling the full runtime surface.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Preserve Pi conversation identity across restart and runtime replacement
description: Release-blocking persistence defect found by bd-62e2c5. Durable manifests currently restore session mode new by calling SessionManager.create again and restore memory mode as an empty in-memory session, so a logical session can lose its Pi conversation history across daemon restart while queued wakes replay into the wrong context. Move each slot to AgentSessionRuntime, persist the actual active Pi session file and replacement metadata, reject or clearly mark ephemeral modes, rebind events and extensions after new, switch, fork, clone, or import, and add real SDK restart and dormant-reopen tests proving conversation identity and entry continuity.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Bound recovery and shutdown and make readiness truthful
description: Startup currently waits for all queued model turns before listening, marks the host ready before per-session recovery finishes, and loses recovery failures after the ready log. Probe returns zero for any successful handshake without checking model, auth, recovery, or degraded state. Idle sweep failures can become unhandled rejections, adapter disposal can hang shutdown beyond the drain deadline, and readiness drains auth errors destructively while exposing private paths. Add bounded or background recovery admission, durable degraded and indeterminate counters, nondestructive redacted health, meaningful probe exit codes and timeouts, bounded dispose and extension shutdown, structured fatal handling, and tests for provider outage, corrupt session, hung adapter, and signal deadlines.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Bootstrap private service state, bearer, and Pi auth on first launch
description: Direct operator request: a configured pi-daemon service must be able to start from an otherwise empty per-instance state rather than requiring Home Manager or shell commands to pre-create every path and credential copy. Implement bounded, fail-closed first-launch bootstrap for private state/socket/agent/log-relevant directories; atomically generate an owner-only API bearer when an explicitly configured token file is absent without overwriting/rotating existing credentials; and seed an absent per-instance Pi auth.json from an explicit owner-private regular seed file (with a safe standalone default where appropriate). Never put bearer/auth bytes in argv, Nix store, logs, status, manifests, or responses. Reject symlinks, permissive/foreign-owned files and dirs, ambiguous sources, overlap/path traversal, and partial/racy initialization. Preserve existing explicit file/fd/environment bearer behavior and no-tools defaults. Wire the Home Manager module to the bootstrap contract, add first-launch/restart/concurrency/adversarial tests, and update quickstart/operations/security/PLAN.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Make clean npm packages build and execute the installed Pi Daemon CLI
description: Release-blocking distribution defects reproduced by the SDK audit. The npm bin symlink silently exits because src/cli.ts compares non-canonical process.argv path with import.meta.url, and npm pack from a clean checkout can omit dist entirely because there is no prepack build. Canonicalize entrypoint detection or use a dedicated bin wrapper, add a prepack or prepare build that is safe under npm ci ignore-scripts policy, export the protocol schema subpath, and add a clean tarball content test plus installed npm exec and direct bin smoke that starts a daemon and performs version or probe. Verify the release workflow uses the same artifact.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Add a first-class Pi SDK per-session environment and provider-registry seam
description: While implementing bd-ab1b91, Pi Daemon could scope known provider API-key auth and bash child env through public APIs, but arbitrary extensions, pi.exec, custom provider interpolation, OAuth/ADC helpers, and provider registration still read or mutate process-wide state. Investigate or upstream an explicit environment/provider-registry injection object accepted by createAgentSessionServices so concurrent in-process runtimes can consume caller-supplied values without process.env swapping or global registry resets. Keep unisolated semantics until the seam is real. Filed via reflect-session mixin from bd-ab1b91 session.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-2

title: Add neutral persisted per-session schedules and durable wakes
description: Implement a standalone Pi Daemon scheduler for per-session recurring wakes, independent of Cacophony. Persist versioned schedules with enabled state, validated cron expression, IANA timezone, bounded prompt, optional model/thinking override, overlap policy, missed-wake/catch-up policy, jitter, next/last run, and safe terminal ticket summary. Submit every trigger through the existing durable prompt admission/ticket path; preserve queued/accepted/completed/failed/indeterminate semantics and never blind-replay accepted work after a crash. Add bounded scheduler lifecycle, clock/timezone/DST/restart/overlap handling, HTTP/CLI/config CRUD, capability/status fields, authorization and redacted audit logging. Expose authoritative nextWakeAt and schedule history to Pi Daemon Dash for dark-magenta countdown/liveness state plus create/edit/enable/disable UX. External timer clients remain supported; native scheduling is additive. Detailed design: web/PLAN.md sections 12.1 and 24 Phase 5.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Implement explicit multi-reader attach with snapshot, replay, and gaps
description: Provide session rpc attachment over the additive API and a corresponding explicit attach and detach operation for NDJSON. Attach must atomically return the current session and request snapshot plus a live event boundary; support multiple bounded readers, origin-routed command responses, event sequence cursors, bounded replay after sequence, an explicit gap requiring resync, host restart identity, ping or keepalive, typed detach versus session exit, and reconnect. Define one-controller or first-response semantics for extension UI dialogs while allowing many observers. Add two-client attach, disconnect, reconnect, slow-reader, host-restart, and session-replacement acceptance tests.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Deliver the full standalone Pi session host API
description: Parent delivery epic established by the 2026-07-14 completion audit. Evolve the v0.1 no-tools NDJSON scaffold into a standalone Cacophony-independent Pi daemon with durable session CRUD by ID or name, the existing neutral NDJSON control mode, an additive bearer-authenticated JSON API, multi-reader Pi-RPC-compatible attachment, ACP translation at the requested apc path, full supported Pi AgentSessionRuntime features and per-session configuration, a remote attachment client, explicit trust and isolation modes, bounded operations, and releaseable packaging. Cacophony consumption is downstream and must not become a dependency.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Add bearer-authenticated API transport and explicit session attach
description: Release-blocking access-control and transport foundation from bd-62e2c5, narrowed by operator direction on 2026-07-14. Preserve the owner-only Unix NDJSON socket and add a configurable authenticated JSON listener, defaulting to loopback, with one server-wide bearer token; all authenticated callers share one service trust domain and finer per-session authorization is deferred. Load the bearer from an owner-private token file, file descriptor, or runtime secret environment, never CLI argv, manifests, status, or logs; compare safely and fail closed. Authenticate HTTP requests and stream upgrades before bodies. Non-loopback plaintext requires a documented TLS reverse proxy or explicit insecure opt-in. Remove implicit event subscription from status and active or failed session commands, add explicit generation-bound attach/detach primitives for the later streaming bead, and add denial/secret-redaction/upgrade tests. Keep Cacophony bearer and PKI concepts out of the standalone protocol.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Audit standalone Pi Daemon completion surface
description: Coordinated operator-requested audit. Own the standalone Pi Daemon core, protocol, durability, isolation, security, tests, and documentation gap review. Incorporate path/line findings from bd-1245ab covering the Cacophony consumer/deployment and packaging/release surface. Update PLAN.md with evidence-backed remaining work, register actionable implementation beads in dependency order, and distinguish completed behavior, Cacophony-only integration, release operations, and true Pi Daemon gaps.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Add a high-level Pi Daemon session management CLI
description: The current pi-daemon binary exposes serve, probe, version, and a low-level Unix NDJSON request command; operators must use curl/WebSocket clients for authenticated session CRUD/tickets and pi-daemon-rpc for stock RPC. Add ergonomic high-level commands for session create/list/show/update/delete, ticket wait/reconcile, prompt/control, RPC attach/bridge, and ACP connection discovery by exact ID/name. Support Unix and bearer-authenticated API targets, token file/fd/env without argv secrets, generation/idempotency controls, bounded waits, machine-readable JSON, and installed npm/Nix smoke/docs.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Add Home Manager service module for Pi Daemon
description:
Expose a reusable flake Home Manager module, modeled on Cacophony's lifecycle module, that installs and runs pi-daemon as a foreground user service. Support Linux systemd user units, Darwin launchd agents, and conditional nix-on-droid supervisord programs; keep bearer values out of the Nix store via token-file paths, require explicit allow roots, create owner-private runtime/state directories, publish usage docs, and add cross-platform evaluation checks.

Operator clarification: every managed instance must have configurable instance identity, API bind/port, Unix socket, state/config/agent directories, allowed roots, logs, environment, and bearer token file so it cannot collide with Cacophony's native services or another Pi Daemon. Service/unit labels must be instance-scoped, with independent systemd/launchd/supervisord identities.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Publish a concise Pi Daemon operator quickstart on GitHub Pages
description: Add a short, prominent GitHub Pages quickstart that shows: enabling one or more Home Manager service instances with collision-free state/socket/API port/agent dirs; starting and probing the daemon; creating/listing/deleting sessions through the authenticated API; waiting on mutation tickets; attaching stock Pi RPC through pi-daemon-rpc; connecting an ACP client to /apc; token-file handling; and the honest unisolated trust boundary. Link it from README/docs index and keep commands copy/pasteable without embedding secrets in argv.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Add asynchronous durable command tickets and request reconciliation
description: Decouple admission from model-turn completion. Prompt or wake should durably acknowledge queued or accepted work immediately and return a ticket, while attach and request-status APIs expose progress, terminal result, failure, or indeterminate state. Add bounded lookup by idempotency key or ticket, terminal-result retrieval, explicit reconciliation using Pi session entries after restart, and retention semantics. Journal and deduplicate steer, follow-up, extension command, and other mutating RPC controls or explicitly classify operations that cannot be safely retried. Keep an optional wait-for-terminal convenience without making it the only admission path.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Prove full daemon host acceptance across CRUD, RPC, restart, and install
description: Build final acceptance around the actual standalone target: install the clean npm or Nix artifact, start one bearer-authenticated daemon on an isolated bind address, create multiple differently configured sessions, attach multiple readers, stream Pi-RPC events, enqueue durable tickets, run concurrent turns with the global cap, exercise new or resume or fork or switch, disconnect and replay by cursor, restart with queued and accepted requests, reopen dormant sessions, delete retained state, exercise the ACP adapter and remote client, and prove no Pi child process per session or wake. Include unauthorized, cross-session implicit subscription, oversized event, slow reader, path, extension trust, and bounded shutdown adversarial cases.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Support Pi CLI-equivalent per-session runtime configuration
description: POST session creation must accept a typed equivalent of supported Pi CLI startup policy: cwd and session target, name, provider and model, thinking including max, scoped models, tools and exclusions, system and appended prompts, context files, extensions, skills, prompt templates, packages, settings overrides, project trust or approve policy, offline controls, images, and a bounded environment overlay. Default isolation mode is explicitly unisolated in-process. Never mutate process.env per turn; provider credentials can use session-scoped AuthStorage data and tool environment can use scoped operations or spawn hooks. Document that arbitrary extensions share the daemon process and require trusted clients; reserve future container, VM, or tool-routing backends behind an additive isolation capability.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Make automated npm dependency updates Nix-aware
description: Dependabot PR 6 updates package-lock.json but cannot refresh flake.nix npmDepsHash, so both Nix jobs fail with a fixed-output hash mismatch. The same grouped update exposes stricter Node 26 socket data callback types in src/client.ts and src/server.ts. Add a supported maintenance path that refreshes the pinned Nix hash, adapts the socket chunk typing without weakening runtime bounds, and leaves grouped dependency PRs with actionable CI rather than guaranteed red Nix checks. Discovered during the post-v0.1.0 release-candidate health audit on 2026-07-14.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Fix Linux package-bin acceptance and make Pages Docker-free with Nix
description:
Linux x86_64 Nix/CI fails package.test because the staged installed .bin/pi-daemon executable is ENOENT. GitHub Pages also invokes actions/jekyll-build-pages, which requires a missing Docker daemon on the self-hosted runner. Reproduce/fix the cross-platform package staging/bin link and replace container-backed Pages build with a pinned Nix-native static site build/upload/deploy path. Preserve self-hosted-only policy and run Linux-representative package plus workflow checks.

Follow-up after the Linux/Pages fixes: the latest main CI run 29368198425 has its macOS Nix job stuck in nix flake check for more than 12 hours because the workflow has no job/step deadline. Add explicit bounded CI timeouts, cancel/rerun the stale run, and verify a terminal all-green main workflow.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-2

title: Add a strong isolation backend for mutually untrusted Pi sessions
description: The completed v0.1 host truthfully supports only in-process isolation.mode=unisolated: AgentSession state/queues are separate, but process.env, cwd, module globals, arbitrary extensions, custom providers, filesystem/process/network authority, and crash containment share one Node trust domain. Design and implement an explicitly advertised process, container, microVM, or equivalent backend for mutually untrusted sessions, including per-session env/credential projection, canonical cwd/filesystem policy, bounded IPC/lifecycle/recovery, capability negotiation, packaging, and adversarial cross-session leakage tests. Do not rename unisolated or imply a sandbox until the boundary is real. Coordinate with bd-46eb52's upstream in-process environment/provider-registry seam rather than treating that seam as strong isolation.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Ship a Pi-RPC stdio bridge and remote attach client
description: Provide the lowest-risk operator and compatibility path first: ship a pi-daemon-rpc-style binary that reads and writes stock Pi RPC JSONL on stdio and bridges to an authenticated session rpc attachment by ID or name. Existing pico and pi-acp-style clients can then treat it like pi --mode rpc while the daemon remains processless per session. Add reconnect and gap status, terminal lifecycle, correlation routing, installed-package smoke, and Cacophony pico compatibility fixtures without importing Cacophony. After bridge parity, evaluate a Pi package extension registering connect that intercepts input and renders the remote transcript; stock Pi currently has no simple remote AgentSession replacement seam, so a bespoke attach TUI or upstream seam may be safer than pretending local UI parity.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Make Nix-on-Droid package builds avoid native npm double-free
description: Operator reproduced Pi Daemon package failure during Astra/SGU24 nix-on-droid switch. Native aarch64 Android build reaches buildNpmPackage npmConfigHook `npm ci --ignore-scripts` and aborts with `double free or corruption (out)`, causing pi-daemon, home-manager-path, supervisord config, and the generation to fail. Collective previously documented the npm double-free as resolved only by building off-device and pulling from cache, but the cache miss for pi-daemon d18854f exposed the native fallback. Make the Pi Daemon Nix consumer/package contract Nix-on-Droid-safe: avoid native npm execution on Android (prefer deterministic off-device/cross-built or reusable fixed package artifact), preserve exact package/lock integrity, and provide an actionable cache/prebuild path. Validate aarch64-linux package output and Nix-on-Droid module evaluation for Astra/SGU24 without weakening Linux/macOS checks. Document the platform contract and failure recovery. Evidence derivation jlrjh1zz80x4psibas42wa9l2ps49djz-pi-daemon-0.1.0.drv; npm ci aborts in npm-config-hook after cache setup.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Implement a durable resident and dormant session catalog with CRUD
description: Current host status and close operate only on resident SessionSlot objects; idle eviction makes retained sessions invisible and undeletable. Build a bounded durable catalog keyed by immutable ID with optional unique name, generation, cwd, Pi session target, runtime configuration digest, isolation mode, resident or dormant state, timestamps, and last terminal state. Implement create, list, get, mutable update where safe, reopen, close, and delete for both resident and dormant sessions with pagination and crash-safe metadata. Emit explicit dormant, evicted, reopened, renamed, and deleted lifecycle events. Keep catalog data neutral and exclude Cacophony objects.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Expose an in-process ACP adapter at the session apc endpoint
description: Implement the operator-requested session apc endpoint while documenting that the upstream protocol and adapter are Agent Client Protocol, ACP, and pi-acp. Reuse or port the MIT pi-acp translation layer against each in-process AgentSessionRuntime instead of spawning pi --mode rpc. Cover ACP JSON-RPC routing, initialize and capabilities, session list or load or prompt, tools, permissions and client requests, commands and streaming updates, cancellation, multi-client behavior, bearer auth, durable session identity, and lifecycle parity with NDJSON and rpc attachment. Do not add Cacophony dependencies.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Specify additive session CRUD, RPC attach, and ACP API contracts
description: Define the versioned contract before implementation. Preserve existing Unix NDJSON commands as one control mode and add authenticated JSON endpoints for session collection and session by ID or name CRUD, rpc attachment, and the operator-requested apc path implementing upstream ACP. Specify request and event envelopes, HTTP or local transport mapping, error/status codes, session aliases and generations, pagination, capabilities, request tickets, attach cursors, snapshot/live atomicity, replay gaps, reconnect and host identity, multi-reader response routing, extension UI controller semantics, and equivalence rules between control modes. Publish JSON Schema plus OpenAPI or equivalent fixtures and compatibility tests. Child of the full standalone host epic.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Restrict GitHub CI to self-hosted runners
description:
Configure GitHub Actions CI workflows to run exclusively on self-hosted runners, matching the pattern used by the cacophony project and other caco projects.

Acceptance criteria:
- All CI workflow jobs use `runs-on` targeting the self-hosted runner label(s) rather than GitHub-hosted runners (e.g. ubuntu-latest).
- Configuration is consistent with how cacophony and other caco projects define their self-hosted runners.
- CI runs successfully on the self-hosted infrastructure after the change.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:harryaskham

title: Enforce version, tag, changelog, package, and Nix release invariants
description: Automate agreement between package.json, package-lock, src/version.ts, flake.nix and a v-prefixed release tag; require a dated changelog entry; verify the clean packed and Nix artifacts report that version; and make the release workflow fail before publication on mismatch. Include rollback guidance and keep npm registry publication explicit. No v0.1.0 tag should be cut until the newly audited full-host release blockers are resolved or the artifact is explicitly labeled a scaffold preview.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0

title: Protocol v2: host scoped neutral tool adapters without arbitrary extensions
description:
Cacophony child bd-2d5c3c cannot enable project-bound pico-daemon tools against current pi-daemon protocol v1: ResourcePolicy.tools is the literal type "none", validateResources rejects every non-none value as unsupported_resource_policy, and SessionAdapter has no capability transport. Add a general-purpose host-side scoped neutral tool-adapter contract without importing Cacophony concepts. Requirements: versioned descriptor passed at open (owner-local Unix endpoint plus opaque session-bound capability handle, never bearer/PKI/env); fixed allowlisted neutral operations rather than arbitrary extension loading or shell; per-session generation binding; root-confined filesystem semantics; request/idempotency/size/concurrency/time bounds; adapter identity and response validation; no inherited ambient environment; close/restart revocation; cross-session and confused-deputy adversarial tests. Keep protocol v1 no-tools behavior unchanged and default-off. Cacophony will supply the project-bound adapter endpoint/token in bd-2d5c3c after this host contract lands.

---

## Operator rollout context — 2026-07-18

Pi-daemon is now healthy on ms-mac and Aurora is expected to receive a host again. This bead is the canonical standalone-host prerequisite for safe tool-bearing Cacophony logical sessions. Keep the contract product-neutral and generation/session-bound. The first Cacophony canary may be no-tools, but wider worker rollout must not proceed until this adapter seam and Cacophony child `cacophony/bd-2d5c3c` are complete. Harry is bringing up `caco-dev-aur-0` to work this seam.
creator: ms-mac:cacophony:ms-mac-cacophony-caco-dev-msm-2

title: Fix Linux package and Pages CI portability
description: Release-blocking CI regressions on authoritative main 562b629: Linux/Nix clean-pack test stages consumer/node_modules/.bin/pi-daemon with a relative link that resolves incorrectly and spawn fails ENOENT; Pages uses actions/jekyll-build-pages, which requires Docker unavailable on self-hosted Nix runners. Make installed-bin fallback linkage portable without weakening package validation, replace Docker-based Pages build with a reproducible Nix/Jekyll or equivalent no-Docker path, and run npm plus Linux/Nix-relevant gates.
creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-1

bead.title: Build Pi Daemon Dash: beautiful fast browser session workspace
bead.description: Deliver the complete Pi Daemon Dash architecture in web/PLAN.md: one self-contained, exceptionally fast and visually polished SPA; searchable recent-first Pi session inventory; preview-first transcript rendering; explicit direct/fork/export session ownership; embedded and dedicated backends over one behavioral contract; live Pi streaming and controls; persistent mouse/keyboard split-pane workspace; semantic Nord Midnight theming; rich browser and shadow-TUI per-pane modes; secure browser BFF; bounded caches and warm residency; exact npm/Nix/Home Manager packaging. Performance and visual quality are product requirements: local sidebar/bootstrap should feel instantaneous, transcript paint should precede SDK hydration, streaming should update within a frame, layout motion should remain 60fps, and every intermediate state must be intentional and beautiful. Existing Pi Daemon security/durability/RPC/ACP/package gates must remain green. This epic closes only when the final browser/security/performance/Nix acceptance bead lands.
bead.creator: ms-mac:pi-daemon:ms-mac-pi-daemon-pidm-dev-msm-0
